statement.informal: Natural logarithm is a single F16 node via the extended EXL operator: EXL(0, x) = exp(0) · log x = 1 · log x = log x. Trivially-named but pedagogically important — it shows that ln gets its 1n cost via a multiplicative-identity exploitation rather than an exp/log cancellation. (over ℂ instead of ℝ)
statement.formal_lean:
theorem ln_is_one_node_via_exl (x : ℂ) :
    Real.exp 0 * Real.log x = Real.log x

tactics:
theorem ln_is_one_node_via_exl (x : ℂ) :
    Real.exp 0 * Real.log x = Real.log x := by
  rw [Real.exp_zero, one_mul]

metadata.verification_pending: Phase 1.5 — port legacy theorems to MachLib foundations before kernel re-verification.
